title: ## STANDARD FOR SPICES DERIVED FROM DRIED OR DEHYDRATED FRUITS AND BERRIES SMALL CARDAMOM CXS 357-2024

sections.section: ## STANDARD FOR SPICES DERIVED FROM DRIED OR DEHYDRATED FRUITS AND BERRIES SMALL CARDAMOM CXS 357-2024
sections.summary: Document title: 'Standard for Spices Derived from Dried or Dehydrated Fruits and Berries — Small Cardamom (CXS 357‑2024)', adopted in 2024.

Applies to dried or dehydrated small cardamom (as defined in Section 2.1) offered for direct consumption, as a food‑processing ingredient, or for repackaging, and explicitly excludes product intended for industrial processing.

Defines dried/dehydrated small cardamom as the dried fruits of Elettaria cardamomum and provides the product definition table and permitted styles (see 2.1–2.3), including optional sizing; other distinct styles are allowed if labelled accordingly.
Defines the product as dried or dehydrated small cardamom obtained from the dried fruits of Elettaria cardamomum (L.) Maton and presents a table of common, trade and scientific names.

Lists permitted styles for small cardamom: whole unopened capsules/pods, opened capsules/pods (opening ≤ one‑third of suture length with seeds), seeds, powdered seeds, and powdered whole capsules/pods, and allows other distinctly different styles if labelled accordingly.
Heading only: optional sizing provision indicated but no substantive sizing requirements are provided in this section.

States the product must conform to composition requirements in Annex I and sets quality factors: characteristic odour, flavour and colour free from foreign odours/flavours (notably rancidity and mustiness), compliance with chemical and physical requirements in Annex I, and that allowed defects must not impair general appearance, keeping quality or package presentation; classification provisions apply if traded as classified.
Composition clause: requires the product described in Section 2 to conform to the requirements contained in Annex I.

Sets out quality factors for dried/dehydrated small cardamom, covering sensory attributes (odour, flavour, colour), required chemical and physical characteristics per Annex I, and optional classification rules; allowed defects must not impair appearance, keeping quality or package presentation.

Requires the product to have a characteristic odour, flavour and colour (which may vary by geoclimatic factors) and to be free from any foreign odour, flavour or colour, especially rancidity and mustiness.

Mandates that dried/dehydrated small cardamom comply with chemical and physical requirements in Annex I (Table A1 and Table A2) and that permitted defects must not adversely affect overall appearance, quality, keeping quality or package presentation.
States that if the product is traded as classified, the provisions in Annex I apply as minimum requirements.
Permits the use of anticaking agents listed in Table 3 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192-1995) in ground/powdered forms of the product conforming to this standard.

Requires compliance with Codex maximum levels for contaminants and toxins (CXS 193-1995), the Code of Practice for prevention and reduction of mycotoxins in spices (CXC 78-2017) and other relevant Codex texts, and with Codex-established maximum residue limits for pesticides.

sections.section: 5.1 The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum levels of the General standard for contaminants  and  toxins  in  food  and  feed (CXS 193-1995), 2   the Code of  practice  for  the  prevention  and reduction of mycotoxins in spices (CXC 78-2017) 3   and other relevant Codex texts.
sections.summary: Requires products to comply with maximum levels specified in the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193-1995), the Code of Practice for mycotoxins in spices (CXC 78-2017) and other relevant Codex texts.

sections.section: 5.2 The products covered by this standard shall comply with the maximum residue limits for pesticides established by the Codex Alimentarius Commission.
sections.summary: States that the products must comply with Codex Alimentarius Commission maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with Codex hygiene texts (General Principles of Food Hygiene CXC 1-1969, Code for Low-Moisture Foods CXC 75-2015 Annex III and other relevant texts) and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21-1997.
Requires containers to be filled as fully as practicable without impairing quality and to be consistent with a proper declaration of contents.

Requires labelling in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1-1985) and adds specific provisions including product name, style indication, optional trade/variety names, and country/region/year of harvest information.
Specifies that the product name must use the common name from Section 2.1, may include a style indication per Section 2.2, and may list trade name, variety or cultivar.

sections.section: 8.2.1 The common name of the product shall be as described in Section 2.1.
sections.summary: States that the common name of the product shall be as described in Section 2.1.

sections.section: 8.2.2 The name of the product may include an indication of the style as described in Section 2.2.
sections.summary: Allows the product name to include an indication of style as described in Section 2.2.

sections.section: 8.2.3 Trade name, variety or cultivar may be listed on the label.
sections.summary: Permits listing of trade name, variety or cultivar on the label.
States that labelling of non‑retail containers should follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346-2021).
